items.mistake: Treating the pretext task as the final product
items.fix: Treat pretext accuracy as a training signal only; evaluate and deploy using downstream task metrics (classification F1, retrieval recall, generation quality) on labeled or human-judged benchmarks.
items.why: A model can excel at rotation prediction yet fail on object recognition if representations encode angle shortcuts instead of semantic content.
items.example: Report ImageNet linear-probe accuracy after SimCLR pretrain, not just contrastive loss on the pretext batch.

items.fix: Reserve SSL for methods with an explicit prediction loss on pseudo-labels derived from data. Pure clustering (k-means) or dimensionality reduction without a pretext target is unsupervised, not self-supervised.
items.why: Conflating terms hides whether gradients come from a surrogate classification/regression objective.
items.example: PCA on pixels is unsupervised; predicting masked patches from context is self-supervised.

items.mistake: Assuming SSL eliminates the need for labels entirely
items.fix: Plan a downstream phase: fine-tuning with human labels, few-shot prompting, or retrieval over a labeled index for production tasks.
items.why: Pretext objectives rarely match business KPIs directly — e.g., next-token loss does not equal helpful chat behavior without further alignment.
items.example: GPT pretraining is SSL; instruction tuning on human-rated dialog adds supervised signal for deployment.
